questions.clue: After Pete Best, The Beatles went with him as their drummer when their manager's pick Johnny Hutchinson said no thanks
When Brian Epstein signed The Beatles, this man was the drummer
The lads honed their craft early on in this German city, sometimes playing 30 sets a week
\"Let me take you down, 'cause I'm going to" this song, recorded in 2 different speeds & keys & combined for an eerie effect
choices: Lucy in the Sky with Diamonds

questions.clue: Jesus & Gandhi were 2 of John's picks that didn't make it onto the celeb-filled cover of this 1967 album
Alliterative name for a line of people who pass pails of water to help put out a fire
(Morgan Freeman reads another clue.) 1 of the amazing sights visited in "The Bucket List" is this fortification stretching from Gansu to Hebei
(Hi, I'm Sean Hayes.) This Islamic tomb on the Yamuna River in India leads Carter and Edward to a discussion of funeral types in "The Bucket List\"
This character "appeared on the sidewalk with a bucket of whitewash and a long-handled brush\"
(Rob Reiner starts us off.) My movie "The Bucket List" stars Morgan Freeman & this acting legend whom I also directed when he played Col. Jessup in "A Few Good Men\"
Often found in Shakespeare's plays, the stage direction "exeunt" means the actors do this
\"Avian" term for the offstage area where the actors await their cues
(Jimmy and the rest of the Clue Crew, on stage at the St. James Theatre.) It's the two-word term for the event after a play when the actors appear to take their bows
(Oooooooklahoma! Where Sofia's standing on the set.) It's the term for raising or lowering scenery above the stage; it's also what Mary Martin did in "Peter Pan\"
This kind of backdrop that often represents the sky is known as a "cyc" for short

dailyDouble.clue: They're sometimes called grasslands or savannahs, but in "God Bless America", they're called these
